excluded_defs.name: FeatureSupport
excluded_defs.reason: Investigated for provider detection patterns but it only handles array support (Postgres/ClickHouse); not suitable for ANSI string detection

exploration_log.aha_moment: Since `AddParameter` receives the `IDbCommand`, and `IDbCommand.Connection` is accessible, the provider can be detected at parameter-setting time rather than construction time. OleDb and ODBC connections are the canonical ANSI-only providers in ADO.NET.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Only handles array support for Postgres/ClickHouse, not ANSI string detection

exploration_log.hindsight: The task says 'provider factory' which might imply using DbProviderFactories.GetFactory(connection) or (connection as DbConnection).GetType(). My approach checks the connection type name which is simpler but equivalent for practical purposes.

alternatives: Add a nullable backing field for IsAnsi to distinguish 'not set' from 'explicitly false'
Use DbProviderFactory via (connection as DbConnection)?.GetType() reflection
key_decisions.decision: Check connection type name at AddParameter time and OR with existing IsAnsi value
key_decisions.reasoning: Minimal change to the public API while correctly detecting ANSI-only providers at the point where the connection is available. Using bool OR means if user explicitly set IsAnsi=true, it stays true.

search_sequence.reasoning: Task description names this file and the IsAnsi behavior

solve_notes: Modified `AddParameter` to detect whether the connection is an ANSI-only provider (OleDb/ODBC by type name) and compute an `effectiveIsAnsi` value that is true if either `IsAnsi` is already set or the provider requires ANSI strings. Added a private `IsAnsiProvider` helper for the detection logic.
task_text:
Fix DbString defaulting to ANSI for Unicode connection strings

`DbString` defaults `IsAnsi = false`, but when the connection string
specifies an ANSI-only provider, the parameter is still sent as
`DbType.String` (Unicode) causing implicit conversion. Fix `DbString`
to detect the provider and set `IsAnsi` appropriately based on the
connection's provider factory.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: FeatureSupport
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an AI agent would look at FeatureSupport to see if provider detection already exists and whether to reuse it for ANSI detection

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set contains the edited methods. `FeatureSupport` is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent would search for existing provider-detection patterns before writing its own. Seeing FeatureSupport.Get() quickly shows it only handles array support and is not suitable, preventing the agent from trying to extend it or reuse it incorrectly.
